revision.comment: Text replacement - "白河郡}}" to "|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt==Shirakawa District}}"
revision.text:
'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt==Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt==Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り}}) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り}}) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏}}

[[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-qq]] [[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-qq]] [[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[ja:白河国造]]

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り}}) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り}}) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏}}

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り}}) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り}}) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏}}

[[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[ja:白河国造]]

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り}}) - '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り}}) - '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏}}

[[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[ja:白河国造]]

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

[[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:白河国造]]

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).

[[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:白河国造]] [[Category:New]]

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).

[[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Fukushima Prefecture history]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]] <!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko-->[[Category:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[ja:白河国造]] [[Category:New]]

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja||西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏}}

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏}}

== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=draft}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=draft}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=draft}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町|12=draft}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩|12=draft}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|12=draft}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城|12=draft}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=draft}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=draft}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群|12=draft}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=draft}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|12=draft}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り|12=draft}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|12=draft}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=draft}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=draft}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|12=draft}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り|12=draft}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=draft}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=draft}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=draft}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=draft}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=draft}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=draft}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=draft}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=draft}}

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|12=draft}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡|12=draft}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|12=draft}}.

=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩|12=draft}}.

After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城|12=draft}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=draft}}, which guarded the barrier.

revision.text:
'''白河国造'''（しらかわのくにのみやつこ）は[[令制国]]での[[陸奥国]][[白河郡|白河郡（しらかわのこおり）]]（現[[福島県]][[白河市]]、[[西白河郡]]、[[東白川郡]]及び[[石川郡 (福島県)|石川郡]]）辺りにあった[[#支配領域|白河国]]を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」によれば、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]5年に天降天由都彦命11世孫である'''鹽伊乃己自直 '''（しほいのこじのあたい）を白河国造に定めたことに始まるとされる。
=== 氏族 === * 玉祖氏と同族で[[東山道]]系の国造では、[[信夫国造]]、[[染羽国造]]、[[伊久国造]]、[[思太国造]]の同族とされる。 * 井上氏など子孫あり。慶長5年（1600年）に伊野郷（現棚倉町の一部）で検地が行われており、この地の有力豪族であった井上信濃守、井上和泉守、井上備後など井上姓を名乗る者がおよそ10名ほど検地表に記載されている。
=== 史跡 ===
* 福島県[[東白川郡]][[棚倉町]]字宮下に鹽伊乃己自直を祀った伊野地蔵尊がある。この地蔵が置かれた地にかつて鹽伊乃己自直が居住していたとされており、当時は小さな祠や鳥居のようなものがあったという。しかし、時代が下るにつれて神仏習合の考え方が広まり、本来は神祇信仰の対象であった鹽伊乃己自直が仏教信仰の対象である地蔵へと姿を変え、現在までその功績が伝承されている。 * 鹽伊乃己自直の墓とされる「馬場古墳」が馬場[[都々古別神社]]の後方小丸山にある<ref>{{Cite book |和書|title=棚倉町大字沿革誌|date=|year=1980|publisher=歴史春秋社|last=棚倉町教育委員会|page=4}}</ref>。
=== 地名 === 福島県[[東白川郡]][[棚倉町]]ホームページによると、鹽伊乃己自直が自ら鋤や鍬を取り、荒れ果てた原野を切り拓き民生に尽くしたことから、地元の民は感謝の意味を込めてこの地を伊乃そして後には転化して入野とも呼ぶようになったという。また、同町の一部は明治初期までは伊野郷伊野上村、伊野下村など命の名に由来する地名が使われていたことが多くの文献で確認できることからも、同町と鹽伊乃己自直は深い関りがあることが窺える。尚、現在の町名は旧[[棚倉藩]]名に由来している。
== 本拠 ==
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''白河国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[令制国]]の[[陸奥国]]'''[[白河郡]]'''、現在[[福島県]][[白河市]]、[[西白河郡]]、[[東白川郡]]及び[[石川郡]]に相当する。
律令制下に入って、[[養老]]2年（718年）から養老6年（722年）または[[神亀]]元年（724年）3月にかけての短期間であるが陸奥国から[[石背国]]へ分割された経緯を持つ当地には、[[ヤマト王権]]の及ぶ北限の地として[[蝦夷]]を防ぐ[[白河の関]]があったとされ、[[大和朝廷の北進]]を代表する[[多賀城]]築城後はそこを守備した[[白河団]]の根拠地となった。
== 氏神など == * [[白河神社]] - 社伝によれば成務5年に鹽伊乃己自直を祀ったとされる。白河の関があったとされる福島県白河市旗宿関ノ森120番に鎮座する。 * [[白河舟田・本沼遺跡群]] - 同遺跡群に含まれる[[白河舟田・本沼遺跡群#下総塚古墳|下総塚古墳]]は6世紀後半の築造と推定され、白河国造の墓との説もある。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] **（[[石背国]]：[[中通り]]）'''白河関'''：白河（白河郡） - [[石背国造|石背]]（[[岩瀬郡]]）- [[阿尺国造|阿尺]]（[[安積郡]]）- [[信夫国造|信夫]]（[[信夫郡]]） **（[[石城国]]：[[浜通り]]）'''菊多関'''（[[勿来関]]）：[[道奥菊多国造|菊多]]（[[菊多郡]]）- [[石城国造|石城]]（[[磐城郡]]）- [[染羽国造|染羽]]（[[標葉郡]]）- [[浮田国造|浮田]]（[[宇多郡]]）- [[伊久国造|伊久]]（[[伊具郡]]）- [[思国造|思]]（[[志田郡]]） **:※ 南から北への順。石背国に属した[[会津郡]]と石城国に属した[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]の郡名を負う国造はいない。 * [[白河結城氏]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらかわのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:陸奥国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:福島県の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[白河国造]] to [[Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Shirakawa District, Mutsu Province|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Shirakawa District|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shida District) - * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{translated page|ja|白河国造|version=90753240|comment=Imported full ja history}}
