=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seimu|qq=}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|qq=}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsandō|qq=}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanagura, Fukushima|qq=}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanagura Domain|qq=}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Province|qq=}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagajō|qq=}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|qq=}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Province|qq=}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadōri|qq=}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Province|qq=}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamadōri|qq=}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|白河国造|version=90753240|comment=Imported full ja history}}

This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|ja|白河市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|西白河郡|1=Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|lt=Nishishirakawa District|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|ja|東白川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|ja|石川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa District, Fukushima|qq=}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukushima Prefecture|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seimu|qq=}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|qq=}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsandō|qq=}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|ja|棚倉町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanagura, Fukushima|qq=}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanagura Domain|qq=}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Province|qq=}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagajō|qq=}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|qq=}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Province|qq=}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadōri|qq=}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Province|qq=}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamadōri|qq=}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|ja|棚倉藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanagura Domain|qq=}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Province|qq=}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}. After the construction of {{ill|Tagajō|ja|多賀城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagajō|qq=}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|ja|白河舟田・本沼遺跡群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group|qq=}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} ** ({{ill|Iwasu Province|ja|石背国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Province|qq=}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|ja|中通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadōri|qq=}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Ishikami Province|ja|石城国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Province|qq=}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|ja|浜通り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamadōri|qq=}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|qq=}}

This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).

{{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|qq=|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

<!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Wikidata entry [[d:" to "Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|qq=|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}
{{translated page|ja|白河国造|version=90753240|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|qq=|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|qq=|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|qq=|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

<!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11579960}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.

The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

<!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11579960}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

{{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}
