revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}
{{translated page|ja|白河国造|version=90753240|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 5]]

After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

<!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

{{wikidata link|Q11579960}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==

The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ([[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]]: [[:en:Nakadōri|Nakadōri]]) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11579960}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Shirakawa District]], within [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. This area is now part of [[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]], [[:en:Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|Nishishirakawa District]], [[:en:Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima]], and [[:en:Ishikawa District, Fukushima|Ishikawa District, Fukushima]] in modern [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]].
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], in the 5th year of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]]'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]], such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In [[:en:Tanagura, Fukushima|Tanagura, Fukushima]], a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former [[:en:Tanagura Domain|Tanagura Domain]].
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of [[:en:Iwase Province|Iwasu Province]] from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. After the construction of [[:en:Taga Castle|Tagajō]], Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * [[:en:Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group]]: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

{{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ([[:en:Iwaki Province (718)|Ishikami Province]]: [[:en:Hamadōri|Hamadōri]]) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}

=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|en|Tanagura Domain|WD=Q909259|lt=Tanagura Domain}}.

=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwase Province|en|Iwase Province|WD=Q1166897|lt=Iwasu Province}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:白河国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11579960]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|en|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155789|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|en|Shirakawa, Fukushima|WD=Q750587|lt=Shirakawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|en|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155789|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|en|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155899|lt=Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155869|lt=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}.
== Overview ==
=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, Fukushima|WD=Q1155858|lt=Tanagura, Fukushima}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|en|Tanagura Domain|WD=Q909259|lt=Tanagura Domain}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwase Province|en|Iwase Province|WD=Q1166897|lt=Iwasu Province}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}. After the construction of {{ill|Taga Castle|en|Taga Castle|WD=Q870867|lt=Tagajō}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|en|Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|WD=Q11579979|lt=Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ({{ill|Iwase Province|en|Iwase Province|WD=Q1166897|lt=Iwasu Province}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|en|Nakadōri|WD=Q5367907|lt=Nakadōri}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province (718)|WD=Q1192809|lt=Ishikami Province}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|en|Hamadōri|WD=Q845466|lt=Hamadōri}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}
{{translated page|ja|白河国造|version=90753240|comment=Imported full ja history}}

This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|en|Tanagura Domain|WD=Q909259|lt=Tanagura Domain}}.

=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.

The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|en|Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|WD=Q11579979|lt=Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.

'''Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (白河国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}} of the Shirakawa region, an area corresponding to {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|en|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155789|lt=Shirakawa District}}, within {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}. This area is now part of {{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|en|Shirakawa, Fukushima|WD=Q750587|lt=Shirakawa, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|en|Nishishirakawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155789|lt=Nishishirakawa District}}, {{ill|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|en|Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155899|lt=Higashishirakawa District, Fukushima}}, and {{ill|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|en|Ishikawa District, Fukushima|WD=Q1155869|lt=Ishikawa District, Fukushima}} in modern {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}.

=== Ancestors === According to the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, in the 5th year of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}'s reign, '''Shioinokoji no Atae''' (鹽伊乃己自直), the 11th-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|天由都彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenoyutsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
=== Clan === * The clan was related to the {{ill|Tamanoya clan|ja|玉祖氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and shared kinship with other Kuni no Miyatsuko clans along the {{ill|Tōsandō|en|Tōsandō|WD=Q1334011|lt=Tōsandō}}, such as {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}}, {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}}, and {{ill|Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思太国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Descendants include the Inoue family. In the 5th year of Keichō (1600), land surveys in the Ino area (now part of Tanagura Town) identified influential local leaders with the surname Inoue, including Inoue Shinano no Kami, Inoue Izumi no Kami, and Inoue Bingo, listed in the survey documents.
=== Historic Sites === * In {{ill|Tanagura, Fukushima|en|Tanagura, Fukushima|WD=Q1155858|lt=Tanagura, Fukushima}}, a statue of Jizō (地蔵), called the Ino Jizō-son, enshrines Shioinokoji no Atae. This Jizō statue replaced earlier shrines and torii, reflecting a shift from kami worship to Buddhist syncretism over time, preserving Shioinokoji no Atae’s legacy. * The "Baba Kofun," believed to be Shioinokoji no Atae's tomb, is located behind the Komaru-yama area of [[Tsutsukowake Shrine]].<ref>{{Cite book |title=棚倉町大字沿革誌 |publisher=歴史春秋社 |year=1980 |author=棚倉町教育委員会 |page=4}}</ref>
=== Place Names === According to Tanagura Town’s official website, Shioinokoji no Atae personally tilled the land, transforming it into fertile farmland. Out of gratitude, the locals referred to the area as "Ino" (伊乃), which later evolved into "Irino" (入野). Historical records confirm that names like Inogō, Inoue, and Inoshimo persisted until the early Meiji period, highlighting the area’s deep connection to Shioinokoji no Atae. The modern name of Tanagura derives from the former {{ill|Tanagura Domain|en|Tanagura Domain|WD=Q909259|lt=Tanagura Domain}}.
== Headquarters ==
=== Domain === The Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as Shirakawa Province, later part of Mutsu Province’s Shirakawa District, now encompassing Shirakawa City, Nishishirakawa District, Higashishirakawa District, and Ishikawa District.
During the Ritsuryō system, the area briefly became part of {{ill|Iwase Province|en|Iwase Province|WD=Q1166897|lt=Iwasu Province}} from the 2nd year of Yōrō (718) to the 6th year of Yōrō (722) or the 1st year of Jinki (724). This region marked the northern boundary of Yamato influence, with Shirakawa Barrier serving as a defensive outpost against the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}}. After the construction of {{ill|Taga Castle|en|Taga Castle|WD=Q870867|lt=Tagajō}}, Shirakawa became a base for the {{ill|Shirakawa Corps|ja|白河団|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Corps|1=Shirakawa Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579957}}, which guarded the barrier.
== Local Deities ==
* {{ill|Shirakawa Shrine|ja|白河神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白河の関|1=Shirakawa Barrier|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1191339}}: According to its tradition, Shioinokoji no Atae was enshrined there in the 5th year of Emperor Seimu’s reign. The shrine is located at 120 Sekinomori, Hatashuku, Shirakawa City, where Shirakawa Barrier was historically situated. * {{ill|Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|en|Shirakawa Funada-Motonuma Sites|WD=Q11579979|lt=Shirakawa Funada-Honnuma Site Group}}: Among this group, the {{ill|Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosozuka Kofun|lt=Shimosōzuka Kofun|ja|下総塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimosōzuka Kofun|qq=}}, dated to the late 6th century, is hypothesized to be the tomb of the Shirakawa Kuni no Miyatsuko.
== Notes == {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}} ** ({{ill|Iwase Province|en|Iwase Province|WD=Q1166897|lt=Iwasu Province}}: {{ill|Nakadōri|en|Nakadōri|WD=Q5367907|lt=Nakadōri}}) ** '''Shirakawa Barrier''': Shirakawa (Shirakawa District) - {{ill|Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石背国造|1=Iwase no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587159}} (Iwase District) - {{ill|Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿尺国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ashaku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657392}} (Asaka District) - {{ill|Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|信夫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Shinobu Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386035}} (Shinobu District) ** ({{ill|Iwaki Province (718)|en|Iwaki Province (718)|WD=Q1192809|lt=Ishikami Province}}: {{ill|Hamadōri|en|Hamadōri|WD=Q845466|lt=Hamadōri}}) ** '''Kikuta Barrier''' ({{ill|Nakoso Barrier|ja|勿来関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakoso Barrier|1=Nakoso no seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400650}}): {{ill|Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|道奥菊多国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michioku Kikuta Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Michinoku Kikuta Kuni-no-Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11641674}} (Kikuta District) - {{ill|Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikami Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} (Ishikami District) - {{ill|Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Sometabe District) - {{ill|Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|浮田国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukita Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487214}} (Uda District) - {{ill|Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} (Igu District) - {{ill|Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|思国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shi Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shida District) *** * Listed south to north. No Kuni no Miyatsuko is associated with the namesake districts of Aizu (Iwasu Province) or Namekata (Ishikami Province).
* {{ill|Shirakawa Yūki Clan|ja|白河結城氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Yūki Clan|1=Shirakawa Yūki Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579975}}
{{translated page|ja|白河国造|version=90753240|comment=Imported full ja history}}
